pages.narration: The Bunnyfield and McFluff Feud: Billy Bob's Tale of Fighting, Families, and Friendship.
In Mingo County, West Virginia, lived Billy Bob's kin. He hopped down to visit them and heard a big din! A din is a loud and terrible fuss. Bunnies were shouting. It was a ruckus!

interaction.prompt: Were the bunnies quiet or loud?

pages.narration: His Bunnyfield cousins were ready to fight because their pet pig named Oinky had vanished at night! One said, who could have taken him? It must be the McFluffs. They live in Kentucky and think they are tough.
Billy Bob said, let us not fight. Let's talk with them and set things right. His Bunnyfield cousins were still in a tizzy. They were shouting and pouting until some went dizzy.
They went to the McFluff's garden and threw stuff around. They pulled up carrots right from the ground. A few even stuffed carrots into a sack, while Billy Bob shouted, PUT THOSE RIGHT BACK!

interaction.prompt: What did they pull from the garden?

pages.narration: The Bunnyfield cousins went home still mad and none of them felt particularly glad.
Back in Kentucky, the McFluffs wanted to fight, and some even said they might bite.
The McFluffs then came up to the Bunnyfields' land and ruined their garden with a ton of sand.
Billy Bob saw Oinky from the corner of his eye. The pig had wandered off and came back to spy. No one had taken him. He had simply run away. He thought causing trouble was one way to play!

interaction.prompt: Who ran away and came back?
interaction.answerReveal: Oinky the pig ran away and came back.

pages.narration: Billy Bob turned to the Bunnyfields and turned to the McFluffs. He shouted, no more fighting. Enough is enough! Let's sit down and talk. Let's drink carrot juice.
Then the Bunnyfields and McFluffs made a big truce. A truce is when you stop fighting and become friends. The Bunnyfields and McFluffs are still pals to the end!

interaction.prompt: At the end, were the families fighting or friends?

pages.narration: From that day on, both families held a reunion every year. They shared carrot juice, laughed, and stayed friends.

interaction.prompt: What was your favorite part of the story?
